title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could be exploited by malicious actors if improperly configured or used to scan unauthorized systems. Additionally, reliance on such tools without proper validation of results could lead to a false sense of security.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure it meets organizational security and compliance requirements.
Educate users on the proper use of the tool and the importance of validating its findings before taking action.
Monitor for any updates or security advisories related to the tool to ensure its safe usage.

title: Nmap through 7.99 does not keep the IPv6 extension-header walk within the captured packet in...
description: Nmap through 7.99 does not keep the IPv6 extension-header walk within the captured packet in ipv6_get_data_primitive (libnetutil/netutil.cc), so the pointer advances past the buffer and the remaining-length computation underflows to a large value. A scanned target or on-path attacker returning a crafted IPv6 response with a truncated extension header can trigger out-of-bounds reads and a crash during raw IPv6 scans.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58058.

title: FFmpeg's RASC video decoder (decode_dlta in libavcodec/rasc.c) performs 32-bit reads and writes...
description: FFmpeg's RASC video decoder (decode_dlta in libavcodec/rasc.c) performs 32-bit reads and writes at the row cursor before the NEXT_LINE row-boundary check and validates the DLTA region in pixel rather than byte units, so a DLTA run on a PAL8 frame can access several bytes past the row allocation. A crafted media stream using the RASC FourCC, decoded by libavcodec, triggers a bitstream-controlled out-of-bounds heap write and adjacent out-of-bounds read, leading to memory corruption.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58049.

title: RustDesk gates incoming control messages on per-capability flags rather than on the session's...
description: RustDesk gates incoming control messages on per-capability flags rather than on the session's authorized connection type, and a file-transfer session does not clear those flags. A peer holding only a valid FileTransfer authorization can inject keyboard and mouse input and reach the unguarded screenshot and display-capture handlers, acting outside its granted scope.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in RustDesk allows unauthorized access to keyboard, mouse input, and display-capture handlers through improper gating of control messages. This can lead to unauthorized remote control, data exfiltration, and potential compromise of sensitive systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately update RustDesk to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-58056.
Restrict access to RustDesk services to trusted networks and implement network segmentation to limit exposure.
Monitor logs and network activity for unusual behavior, particularly around remote control and file transfer sessions.

title: nghttp2's nghttpx proxy through 1.69.0 forwards an HTTP/1.1 Upgrade request that also carries a...
description: nghttp2's nghttpx proxy through 1.69.0 forwards an HTTP/1.1 Upgrade request that also carries a Content-Length header and body onto reusable keep-alive backend connections, re-adding the Upgrade and Connection headers while passing Content-Length verbatim. A backend that resolves the resulting ambiguous message in the attacker's favor enables HTTP request/response smuggling and cross-client response-queue poisoning.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in nghttp2's nghttpx proxy allows HTTP request smuggling and cross-client response-queue poisoning, potentially enabling attackers to bypass security controls, manipulate responses, or launch further attacks on backend systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade nghttp2 to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-58055.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization on HTTP headers to prevent ambiguous messages from being processed.
Monitor and audit backend server logs for signs of HTTP request smuggling or unusual activity.

title: MyBB 1.8.40 does not restrict which usergroup a limited Admin Control Panel user may assign when...
description: MyBB 1.8.40 does not restrict which usergroup a limited Admin Control Panel user may assign when creating or editing users; the user module offers the Administrators group (gid 4) and its datahandler's verify_usergroup() unconditionally returns true. An admin holding only the delegated user-management permission can assign the Administrators group to an account and escalate to the full Administrator permission set.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58054.

title: libssh2 through 1.11.1 reads an attacker-controlled 32-bit attribute count from a publickey...
description: libssh2 through 1.11.1 reads an attacker-controlled 32-bit attribute count from a publickey-subsystem response and uses it in the allocation num_attrs * sizeof(libssh2_publickey_attribute) without bounds checking, so on 32-bit platforms the multiplication overflows to an undersized buffer. A malicious SSH server can then drive the attribute-parsing loop to write past the allocation, causing a heap buffer overflow in a connecting libssh2 client.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58050.

title: Flowise before 3.1.3 validates Custom MCP stdio environment variables against a denylist using a...
description: Flowise before 3.1.3 validates Custom MCP stdio environment variables against a denylist using a case-sensitive comparison, so on Windows, where environment names are case-insensitive, supplying 'node_options' bypasses the NODE_OPTIONS denylist entry. An authenticated user who can configure a Custom MCP node can thereby inject NODE_OPTIONS --require and execute arbitrary code in the Flowise server context.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an authenticated user with specific permissions to bypass environment variable restrictions and execute arbitrary code in the server context. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, or further exploitation of the system.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Flowise version 3.1.3 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the configuration of Custom MCP nodes to trusted users only.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity, especially related to NODE_OPTIONS usage, and implement additional runtime security measures.

title: Gitea act_runner with the Docker backend (through act 0.262.0) passes a workflow's container...
description: Gitea act_runner with the Docker backend (through act 0.262.0) passes a workflow's container.options string to the Docker job container's HostConfig and, when configured with privileged: false, forces only the Privileged flag off while merging options such as --pid=host, --cap-add, and --security-opt unchanged. A user who can run a workflow on a Docker-backed runner can create a job container with host namespaces and broad capabilities and escape to the host as root despite privileged mode being

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58053.

title: 7-Zip for Windows through 26.02 fails to preserve the Mark-of-the-Web when extracting a crafted...
description: 7-Zip for Windows through 26.02 fails to preserve the Mark-of-the-Web when extracting a crafted RAR5 archive, because its guard that suppresses an archive-supplied Zone.Identifier stream matches the exact name 'Zone.Identifier' while a RAR5 STM record named ':Zone.Identifier:$DATA' is not matched and NTFS canonicalizes it to the same stream, overwriting the propagated Internet-zone marker with ZoneId=0. A second STM record named '::$DATA' overwrites the extracted file's default data stream, lett

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in 7-Zip allows crafted RAR5 archives to bypass the Mark-of-the-Web (MOTW) security mechanism. This can lead to malicious files being executed without warnings, potentially enabling attackers to deliver malware or execute arbitrary code. The impact is significant in environments where 7-Zip is used to handle untrusted files.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of 7-Zip as soon as a patch is released to address CVE-2026-58052.
Implement additional endpoint security measures, such as antivirus and EDR solutions, to detect and block malicious files.
Educate employees on the risks of opening untrusted or suspicious archives and enforce policies to handle such files in isolated environments.

title: libssh2 through 1.11.1 grows its publickey list with SSH2_REALLOC but does not zero-initialize...
description: libssh2 through 1.11.1 grows its publickey list with SSH2_REALLOC but does not zero-initialize new entries before parsing populates them, so a parse failure reaching the cleanup path leaves libssh2_publickey_list_free operating on an uninitialized entry. A malicious SSH server offering the publickey subsystem can use a malformed response to make cleanup free an uninitialized, attacker-influenceable attrs pointer in a connecting libssh2 client.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58051.

title: The Frontend File Manager Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authenticated Arbitrary...
description: The Frontend File Manager Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authenticated Arbitrary File Deletion in versions up to and including 23.6. This is due to a case-sensitive bypass of the wpfm_dir_path parameter sanitization in the wpfm_file_meta_update AJAX handler, where supplying WPFM_DIR_PATH in uppercase evades the unset check and is normalized to wpfm_dir_path by sanitize_key() during update_post_meta(), allowing an attacker to overwrite the stored file path with an arbitrary filesyst

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8095.

title: The CONS_HISTORY ioctl handler did not adequately validate the requested history size.  A large...
description:
The CONS_HISTORY ioctl handler did not adequately validate the requested history size.  A large value caused an integer overflow in the buffer size calculation, resulting in a heap allocation smaller than expected.  Subsequent initialization of the buffer wrote beyond the end of the allocation.

An unprivileged local user with access to a vt(4) device can trigger an out-of-bounds write in the kernel, potentially escalating privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an unprivileged local user to exploit an integer overflow in the kernel, leading to an out-of-bounds write. This could potentially result in privilege escalation, enabling the attacker to execute arbitrary code with kernel-level privileges.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches or updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-49416.
Restrict access to vt(4) devices to trusted users only, minimizing the attack surface.
Monitor systems for unusual behavior or signs of exploitation, such as unauthorized privilege escalation attempts.

title: The Frisbii Pay plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data due to...
description: The Frisbii Pay plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data due to missing capability checks on the 'upload_csv' and 'process_batch' functions in all versions up to, and including, 1.8.9. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to upload arbitrary CSV data and overwrite WooCommerce payment tokens, postmeta, and order meta records.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3462.

title: The WP Full Stripe Free plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in versions...
description: The WP Full Stripe Free plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in versions up to, and including, 8.4.3 via the wpfs_update_failed_payment_status AJAX action. The handler is registered through both wp_ajax_ and wp_ajax_nopriv_ hooks and the underlying update_failed_payment_status() function performs no capability check, no nonce verification, and no logged-in check before calling $this->db->updatePaymentByEventId() with attacker-controlled POST parameters. This makes it possi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12432.

description: The Quiz and Survey Master (QSM) – Easy Quiz and Survey Maker plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 11.1.4. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to create, modify, and delete quiz output templates stored in the mlw_quiz_output_templates database table, including storing unsanitized HTML

title: The Dokan: AI Powered WooCommerce Multivendor Marketplace Solution – Build Your Own Amazon, eBay,...
description: The Dokan: AI Powered WooCommerce Multivendor Marketplace Solution – Build Your Own Amazon, eBay, Etsy plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versions up to, and including, 5.0.4 via the 'id' parameter due to missing validation on a user controlled key. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with subscriber-level access and above, to read any other vendor's products — including unpublished draft and pending listings — exposing product names, pr

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with subscriber-level access or higher to exploit an insecure direct object reference (IDOR) flaw, enabling them to access sensitive product information, including unpublished and pending listings. This could lead to data leakage, competitive intelligence gathering, and potential reputational damage for affected businesses.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using WooCommerce
recommended_actions: Update the Dokan plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Implement strict access controls and validate user inputs to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive data.
Monitor user activity logs for suspicious behavior, particularly from accounts with subscriber-level access.

title: The Page Builder by SiteOrigin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting...
description: The Page Builder by SiteOrigin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via panels_data Parameter in all versions up to, and including, 2.34.3 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page. This is possible because the nonce and edit_post capability checks enforced d

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13295.

title: The Spexo theme for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access due to a missing capability...
description: The Spexo theme for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access due to a missing capability check on the activate_plugin function in all versions up to, and including, 2.0.11. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to activate a limited set of plugins.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with minimal privileges (Subscriber-level access) to activate specific plugins, potentially leading to privilege escalation or exploitation of vulnerable plugins. This could compromise the integrity and security of the WordPress site.
recommended_actions: Update the Spexo theme to a patched version as soon as an update is released by the vendor.
Restrict user roles and permissions to the minimum necessary, ensuring that Subscriber-level accounts are limited in number and closely monitored.
Regularly audit installed plugins and remove any unnecessary or unused plugins to reduce the attack surface.

title: The RegistrationMagic – Custom Registration Forms, User Registration, Payment, and User Login...
description: The RegistrationMagic – Custom Registration Forms, User Registration, Payment, and User Login plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass via Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity in all versions up to and including 6.0.8.6. This is due to the PayPal IPN `callback` handler being registered as a nopriv AJAX action with no authentication or nonce requirement, and critically because the handler updates the payment log database row with attacker-controlled POST data — includ

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to bypass authentication and manipulate payment logs by exploiting the PayPal IPN callback handler. This could lead to unauthorized access, fraudulent transactions, and data tampering, posing a significant threat to the integrity of payment systems and user data.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the RegistrationMagic plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily to mitigate risk.
Implement server-side validation and authentication mechanisms for all AJAX actions, especially those handling sensitive data like payment logs.
Monitor payment logs and user activity for signs of unauthorized changes or suspicious behavior, and audit logs for potential breaches.

title: The Gutenverse – WordPress Blocks, Page Builder & Site Editor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable...
description: The Gutenverse – WordPress Blocks, Page Builder & Site Editor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via admin settings in all versions up to, and including, 3.8.0 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with editor-level permissions and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page. This only affects multi-site installations and install

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers with editor-level permissions or higher to inject malicious scripts into pages via the plugin's admin settings. These scripts can execute whenever a user accesses an affected page, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or further exploitation. The issue is limited to multi-site WordPress installations, reducing its overall scope but still posing a significant risk to affected environments.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Gutenverse plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable it temporarily to mitigate the risk.
Restrict editor-level permissions to trusted users only and review user roles and access levels across the WordPress installation.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts and monitor for any unusual activity in the affected WordPress sites.

title: CVE-2026-12786 - A vulnerability has been found in Ezbsystems UltraISO Premium Edition up to 9.76. Affected by this i
description: A vulnerability has been found in Ezbsystems UltraISO Premium Edition up to 9.76. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality in the library bootpt64.sys of the component Kernel Driver. The manipulation leads to improper access controls. Local access is required to approach this attack. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12786.

title:
CVE-2026-52911 - In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ksmbd: scope conn->binding slow
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ksmbd: scope conn->binding slowpath to bound sessions only

When the binding SESSION_SETUP sets conn->binding = true, the flag stays
set after the call so that the global session lookup in
ksmbd_session_lookup_all() can find the session, which was not added to
conn->sessions. Because the flag is connection-wide, the global lookup
path will also resolve any other session by id if asked.

Tighten the global lookup so that the returned session must have this
connection registered in its channel xarray (sess->ksmbd_chann_list).
The channel entry is installed by the existing binding_session path in
ntlm_authenticate()/krb5_authenticate() when a SESSION_SETUP completes
successfully, so this condition is a strict equivalent of "this
connection has been accepted as a channel of this session". Connections
that have not bound to a given session cannot reach it via the global
table.

The existing conn->binding gate for entering the slowpath is preserved
so that non-binding connections keep the fast-path-only behavior, and
the session->state check is unchanged.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-52911.
